question: Will the U.S. Congress still be operating under a continuing resolution (CR) on March 15, 2026?
resolution_criteria:
This question will resolve as Yes if the United States enacts a continuing resolution that funds the United States government through March 15, 2026.

If the United States government enacts complete full-year appropriations funding, applicable through March 15, 2026, this question will immediately resolve as No.
background:
In recent fiscal years, the U.S. Congress has frequently relied on short-term continuing resolutions rather than passing full-year appropriations bills by statutory deadlines. While shutdowns are often avoided through temporary funding extensions, full-year appropriations are sometimes delayed well into the fiscal year due to partisan disagreement, policy riders, or sequencing issues between chambers.

This question assesses whether Congress completes a full-year FY 2026 appropriations package by mid-March, 2026, rather than continuing to govern via temporary funding measures.
